I reject reimage1804_c01 for this branch and move to fallback reimage1804_c02.

Prepared a c02-specific bridge-free conditional Ramsey/T2star/13C plan while pODMR runs. It re-inspects auto__ramsey/ramsey.xml, calculates B and expected 13C Larmor from a 3.875 GHz ms=+1 resonance, keeps det=2 MHz on an 8 us / 51 point grid to avoid FFT aliasing, and records prerequisite gates before any execute.

Raw-exported and reviewed the 3-average autosave of the still-running reimage1804_c02 strong-pi pODMR. The mean signal-only dip remains at 3.875 GHz but has weakened to about 13.5-13.9% versus expected ~22%; reference is not depressed. This odd intermediate snake-order autosave is provenance only; terminal 4-average review remains required. No bridge queue mutation was performed.

Terminal 4-average strong-pi pODMR for reimage1804_c02 completed with healthy counts and a clear signal-only resonance near 3.875 GHz. Raw/fitted-reference depth is about 13.8-14.0%, lower than expected ~22% but far above rejected c01 and not mirrored in reference; Gaussian fit center is 3.876461 GHz (about 0.69 MHz covariance uncertainty), FWHM about 11.2 MHz. Accept c02 as the aligned-NV branch with lower-contrast caveat; T2star/13C remain unmeasured.

reimage1804_c02 accepted as aligned NV branch: The reimage1804_c02 strong-pi pODMR completed with healthy counts. Terminal raw/readout-aware review shows a signal-only resonance near 3.875 GHz: raw/fitted-reference depth about 13.8-14.0%, no matching reference dip, Gaussian center 3.876461 GHz with ~0.69 MHz covariance uncertainty, FWHM ~11.2 MHz. This is lower than the expected ~22% contrast but clearly stronger than rejected c01, so I accept c02 as the aligned-NV branch with a contrast caveat. T2star and 13C are not yet measured.

Copied latest running Ramsey/T2star bridge status at 21:28 EDT: queued/staging empty by direct check, running phase run_experiment_scan_point, 6/8 averages completed, final count text Final = 45.318 kcps, monitor active with no last_error, stop_requested=false, and no hard anomaly. Terminal 8-average Ramsey/T2star scout completed and was raw-exported, plotted, drift-checked, FFT-reviewed, and fit-tested. Counts were healthy (pre-run TrackCenter 43.710 kcps, post-run final 41.020 kcps), scan matched 43 tau points over 0-8 us with det=1.5 MHz and 8 x 50000 reps, and scan-order-aware drift found no flagged averages. Signal readout supports a weak empirical Ramsey oscillation near 1.9 MHz; fit-derived T2* is short/few-us but not a final scalar because early-time handling changes the result. The nominal det carrier is weak and the apparent high-sideband power is ambiguous with an effective carrier shift, so 13C is not established. Recommended next step is bounded weak-pi pODMR resonance refinement before a claim-grade repeat Ramsey/13C test.

c02 Ramsey terminal 8-average review: Terminal 8-average Ramsey/T2star scout completed with healthy counts and no stop/error. Raw export, figure, FFT review, fits, and scan-order-aware drift diagnostic are recorded. The signal readout supports a weak empirical Ramsey oscillation near 1.9 MHz and a short/few-us T2* order, but the scalar T2* is not final because early tau handling changes the fitted value. The nominal 1.5 MHz carrier is weak and the high-frequency power is ambiguous with carrier shift, so 13C is not established. Next recommended experiment is bounded weak-pi pODMR resonance refinement before a claim-grade repeat Ramsey/13C test.

Terminal 4-average weak-pi pODMR refinement completed and was raw-exported, plotted, fit-reviewed, and scan-order drift-checked. Raw/readout-aware review shows a signal-only narrow dip near the prior strong-pi center: fit center 3.876501337 GHz, depth about 11.4%, FWHM about 0.94 MHz; nearest sampled point at 3.876461010 GHz has about 9.0% signal depression and about 0.5% reference depression. This supersedes the provisional 3-average autosave and supports using 3.876501337 GHz as the next Ramsey mw_freq. pODMR itself does not establish 13C.
